scan_metadata.method: Python ast.walk + difflib.SequenceMatcher on ast.dump(annotate_fields=False) [M1 industry spec]
scan_metadata.minimum_size_filter: >=10 body lines OR >=50 AST tokens (SonarQube/jscpd industry default)
excluded_normalization_methods: ast.dump(annotate_fields=True) — Python debugging format per official docs, inflates ratio via field-name strings; 47/47 pairs pass, no discriminative power
source text difflib — character-level sensitive to string literals
token-only (node type sequence) — too coarse for structural similarity
scan_metadata.verification: 4-round 10-verification convergence: bailian + kimi + GLM triple-core + 5 reverse/forward/cross checks + AST normalization deep dive + canonical spec application
scan_metadata.v5_methodology_rationale: Per Python ast.dump documentation, annotate_fields=True 'makes the code impossible to evaluate' (debugging only). Industry tools (PMD CPD, SonarQube, jscpd) all use token-based or annotate_fields=False. Prior counts using annotate_fields=True systematically over-reported duplicates.

v5_total_summary.key_methodology_finding: annotate_fields=True (used in v1-v4) is Python's debugging format per official docs, not a similarity normalization method. Industry tools (PMD CPD, SonarQube, jscpd) all use token-based or annotate_fields=False. Switching to M1 (annotate_fields=False) plus minimum-size filter (>=10 lines OR >=50 tokens) reduced 47 candidates to 10 true duplicates (-80%).

audit_report_gap.previous_inflated_count_rationale: v1-v4 reported 65-66 pairs using annotate_fields=True which systematically over-counts; v5 canonical count is 10
